sections.paragraph:
Ἐπικούρειος, ου, ὁ (Numenius [s. on στρεβλόω 2] 1, 3 p. 63; Alciphron 3, 19, 3; Diog. L. 10, 3; 31; SIG 1227 φιλόσοφος Ἐπικούρειος; IGR IV, 997; Jos., Ant. 10, 277; 19, 32; Just., A II, 15, 3. For the spelling [-ριος Tdf., W-H., Sod.] W-S. §5, 13e; cp. Philo, Poster. Cai. 2) an Epicurean, a follower of Epicurus Ac 17:18 (s. comm.).—RHicks, EncRelEth V 324–30; WdeWitt, E. and His Philosophy ’54; WBarclay, ET 72, ’60, 78–81; 72, ’61, 101–4; 146–49; EAsmis, Epicurus’ Scientific Method ’84; RAC V 681–819.—M-M.

Numenius Numenius , II a.d.—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

IGR IGR = Inscriptiones Graecae ad res Romanas pertinentes—List 3

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

W-H. W-H. = BWestcott/FHort—List 1, beg.

Sod. Sod. = HvSoden, ed. Gk. NT—List 1, beg.

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Epicurus Epicurus , IV–III b.c.—List 5

comm. comm. = commentary, -aries, commentator(s)

EncRelEth EncRelEth = Encyclopaedia of Religion and Ethics—List 6

ET ET = Expository Times—List 6

RAC RAC = Reallexikon für Antike u. Christentum—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 373
